title: What amounts may be deducted from remuneration before employees' tax is calculated?
text: The following amounts may be deducted from remuneration before employees' tax is calculated: pension or provident fund contributions, retirement annuity fund contributions, and so much of any bona fide donation made by the employer on behalf of the employee as does not exceed 5% of that remuneration after taking into account the above allowable deductions.

title: What devices can be used to file an income tax return through the SARS MobiApp?
text: iOS and Android devices, such as cellular phones or tablets, can be used to file an income tax return through the SARS MobiApp.

title: What constitutes a criminal offence relating to filing a return without authority?
text: Criminal offences relating to filing a return without authority occur where a person submits a return or other document to SARS under a forged signature, uses another person's electronic or digital signature in an electronic communication to SARS without that other person's consent and authority, or submits to SARS a communication on behalf of another person without that other person's consent and authority.

title: What is the final tax liability (tax due on assessment) after all deductions?
text: R2 936.00 (shown as negative, indicating a refund due)

title: What is the maximum prison sentence for tax evasion offences?
text: If convicted of criminal offences relating to tax evasion, a person may be subject to a fine or to imprisonment for a period not exceeding five years.

title: What is the primary rebate amount for the 2024/25 year of assessment?
text: The primary rebate is R17 235.

title: What documents should an individual bring when registering for personal income tax at a SARS service centre?
text: When registering, the individual must have all supporting documents, which may include proof of identity, proof of address and proof of banking details. A full list of supporting documentation is available on the SARS website, or you can call the SARS Service Centre on 0800 00 7277 for further information.

title: How can someone have a virtual consultation with a SARS consultant?
text: A virtual consultation with a SARS consultant can be arranged by making an appointment via the SARS website.

title: When was the first payment due for the 2025 year of assessment?
text: The first payment for the 2025 year of assessment was due on or before 30 August 2024.

title: How is employees' tax further reduced after calculation?
text: The employees' tax that is calculated must further be reduced by the amount of any medical scheme fees tax credit or additional medical expenses tax credit that may apply in respect of that employee.

title: What are the local interest exemption thresholds for individuals in the 2025 year of assessment?
text: The local interest exemption thresholds are R23 800 if the individual is below the age of 65, and R34 500 if the individual is 65 years or older.

title: What is the taxable income after deductions, and which tax bracket does it fall into?
text: The taxable income is R271 950 after deducting exempt income and allowable deductions. This taxable income falls within the taxable income bracket of R237 101 – R370 500 according to the statutory rates of tax for the year of assessment ending 28 February 2025.

title: What is the tax rate on retirement fund lump sum benefits that do not exceed R550,000?
text: 0 per cent of the taxable income

title: What happens if the last day of August falls on a Saturday, Sunday or public holiday?
text: If the last day of August falls on a Saturday, Sunday or public holiday, the payment must be made no later than the last business day before the Saturday, Sunday or public holiday.

title: What is included in the assessment issued by SARS after processing an income tax return?
text: The assessment shows the final income tax liability on all of the taxable income earned by the individual for the year of assessment, including but not limited to employment income, business income, taxable capital gains, interest and rental income.

title: What resources are available to help determine if an income tax return must be submitted?
text: An individual unsure whether an income tax return must be submitted may consult the SARS website, which contains an interactive set of questions designed to assist in deciding whether an income tax return is required. Alternatively, they can call the SARS Service Centre on 0800 00 7277 or visit the nearest SARS service centre (by appointment) for assistance.

title: Why is biometric information effective against identity theft and fraud?
text: Biometric information is almost impossible to duplicate, unlike a signature for instance, which makes it an effective prevention against identity theft and fraud.

title: What is the name of the income tax return form for individuals in South Africa?
text: The income tax return applicable to individuals is known as the ITR12 form.

title: What happens if a provisional taxpayer fails to submit the second estimate of taxable income by the end of the fourth month after the end of the year of assessment?
text: If a provisional taxpayer fails to submit the second estimate of taxable income by the end of the fourth month after the end of the year of assessment, they are deemed to have submitted an estimate of Rnil, and the underestimation penalty will be calculated accordingly.

title: From what date do the fixed amount penalties for outstanding income tax returns apply?
text: These penalties are applicable for years of assessment commencing on or after 1 March 2006 where a person has two or more outstanding income tax returns.

title: What information does SARS pre-populate in an income tax return?
text: SARS pre-populates the income tax return with the individual's personal information and information contained in any IRP5/IT3(a) certificate(s), such as employment income, pension fund contributions, and employees' tax deducted. The ITR12 is also pre-populated with certain information available from third party data.

title: How can individuals register for personal income tax if they are not auto registered or registered through their employer?
text: If you are not auto registered or registered through your employer, you can register at your nearest SARS service centre after making an appointment or register for eFiling which will result in you being automatically registered.

title: What is the closing date for electronic submission of 2025 income tax returns for non-provisional taxpayers?
text: On or before 20 October 2025

title: What are the methods available for submitting income tax returns to SARS?
text: Income tax returns may be submitted electronically at a SARS service centre (by appointment), or through eFiling or via the SARS MobiApp, provided the individual is registered for eFiling.

title: What is the understatement penalty percentage for substantial understatement in a standard case?
What is provisional tax and how often must it be paid?
text: Provisional tax is a method of paying income tax on an on-going basis as opposed to paying a large amount once a year on assessment. It is paid twice a year on a six-monthly basis, with a third or 'top-up' payment that can be made to avoid interest.

title: How many business days does an individual have to submit an original return if they disagree with an estimated assessment from SARS?
text: 40 business days from the date of the assessment

title: What deductions can be taken into account when calculating taxable income according to the 2024/25 Income Tax Guide?
text: Allowable tax deductions that can be taken into account include current contributions to a retirement annuity fund, donations to approved public benefit organisations for which a section 18A certificate has been issued, and wear-and-tear allowances. These deductions are subject to certain limitations as legislated.

title: What is the local phone number to contact the SARS Service Centre?
text: If calling locally, the SARS Service Centre can be contacted on 0800 00 7277.

title: What is the normal tax payable before any rebates or deductions?
What is the taxable income amount mentioned in this tax calculation?
What is the tax rate on retirement fund lump sum benefits for taxable income not exceeding R550,000?
text: 0 per cent of the taxable income

title: What is biometric information?
text: Biometric information refers to biological data used to authenticate the identity of an individual by means of fingerprint recognition, facial recognition, vocal recognition and iris or retina recognition.

title: What are some examples of investment income that an individual can be taxed on?
text: Examples of investment income that an individual can be taxed on include interest, foreign dividends, and dividends from a Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT).

title: What are the options for completing and submitting an income tax return electronically?
text: To use eFiling to complete and submit an income tax return electronically, an individual must register as an eFiler. Alternatively, a SARS employee can assist any individual in completing and submitting the income tax return electronically at any SARS service centre (by appointment only).

title: What are the contact hours for the SARS Service Centre on Wednesdays?
text: On Wednesdays, the SARS Service Centre can be called between 9am and 4.30pm South African time.

title: What is considered a business day according to the context?
text: A business day is any day that is not a Saturday, Sunday or public holiday.

title: What is the maximum remuneration from a single employer that allows an individual to not submit an income tax return for the 2025 year of assessment?
text: An individual is not required to submit an income tax return if remuneration from a single employer did not exceed R500 000 for the year of assessment, and employees' tax was correctly withheld in terms of the deduction tables prescribed by the Commissioner for SARS.

title: To whom is income tax payable in South Africa?
text: Income tax is payable to SARS, which acts as the administrator responsible for collecting revenue on behalf of the South African Government. SARS also makes use of withholding agents such as employers that withhold employees' tax from remuneration paid to employees and pay this to SARS on a monthly basis.

title: What social services does the government fund through income tax?
text: The government uses income tax to fund social services such as education, health, security and welfare, with the funding of these services taking place primarily through SARS's administration of revenue collection.

title: What is the deadline for notifying SARS of changes to personal details?
text: A person who has been registered as a taxpayer must communicate to SARS within 21 business days any change that relates to postal address, physical address, representative taxpayer, banking particulars, electronic address, or any other details the Commissioner may require by public notice.

title: What is the definition of 'tax threshold'?
text: The term 'tax threshold' means the maximum amount of taxable income of a person during a year of assessment that would result in no tax being payable by that person taking into account the applicable normal rates of tax and the rebates available in section 6.

title: What is the purpose of employees' tax and provisional tax?
text: Employees' tax and provisional tax exist to help relieve the tax burden that would exist on assessment by spreading the payments over multiple periods instead of one lump sum payable at year-end.

title: How long must an individual retain relevant records and documents for income tax purposes?
text: An individual is required to retain all relevant records, books of account or documents for a period of five years from the date of the submission of the income tax return, or, if no income tax return was required, for five years from the end of the relevant tax period.

title: Do all individuals have to submit income tax returns?
text: No, not all individuals have to submit income tax returns. The guide distinguishes between individuals required to submit an income tax return (section 4.2.1) and individuals not required to submit an income tax return (section 4.2.2).

title: What document must an employer issue to an employee as proof of tax deducted from earnings?
text: An employer must issue an employee with a receipt known as an employees' tax certificate, also known as an IRP5, where employees' tax was deducted or withheld from the employee's earnings.

title: What must the estimate for the second provisional tax payment equal?
text: The estimate for the second payment must be equal to the total liability for the year of assessment.

title: What are examples of criminal offences relating to non-compliance under the TA Act?
text: Examples include failure to register or notify SARS of a change in registered particulars when required, failure to retain records as required, failure or neglect to submit a return or document to SARS or issue a document to a person as required, failure to provide information, documents or material facts to SARS as and when required under a tax Act, and obstructing or hindering a SARS official in carrying out his or her duties.

title: What constitutes a valid signature on an income tax return?
text: The income tax return must be signed by the individual. A signature may comprise an electronic signature, which includes a user ID and access code for electronic submission platforms such as SARS eFiling.

title: How does a vendor access the correction option for a VAT201 Return?
text: A vendor must select the desired return where a correction needs to be made on 'Returns History' from the side menu options under Returns, then select the 'Request for Correction' option to make the second version of the VAT201 Return available for editing.

title: Which vendor categories are most impacted by the VAT rate change from 14% to 15% effective from 1 April 2018?
text: The most impacted are Category B vendors whose tax periods are periods of two months ending on the last day of April 2018. Also impacted are Category D vendors whose tax periods are periods of six months ending on the last day of August 2018, and Category E vendors whose tax periods are periods of twelve months ending on the last day of the year of assessment.

title: What are the two options available after completing the Request for Extension screen?
text: You can select 'Back' to revert to the VAT Work Page or 'Submit' to submit the Request for Extension to SARS.

title: What are the options available to a vendor for requesting a VAT201 Return?
text: A vendor will be allowed to: (i) Request a VAT201 Return for the current tax period and the tax period following current tax period, and (ii) Request and submit a VAT201 Return for a past tax period.

title: What types of entities need to register as an eFiler for VAT purposes?
text: Individuals, tax practitioners, and organisations (such as companies, close corporations, trusts, and exempt institutions) need to register as an eFiler to submit VAT201 returns and make VAT payments via eFiling.

title: What is the mandatory requirement for Field 38 in the VAT201 Return?
text: Field 38 is mandatory if field 36 was completed and must be in full litres. It represents the amount of diesel in respect of eligible purchases (total purchases less non-eligible purchases).

title: What should be reported in Field 7 of the VAT201 Return?
text: The VAT exclusive amount of accommodation supplied not exceeding 28 days must be completed in Field 7. The total value (VAT excluded) in respect of accommodation supplied for a period equal to or less than 28 days (full period) must be reported in Field 7.

title: When is the VDP application number a mandatory field?
text: The VDP application number is a mandatory field if the "Y" block is selected.

title: Which fields should vendors use to deduct input tax for imports in the VAT201 form?
text: For all imports, irrespective of whether the VAT was charged at 14% or 15%, vendors are requested to use Field 14A and 15A of the VAT201 to deduct the input tax.

title: Which banking institutions can vendors use to make EFT payments directly to SARS?
text: Vendors can make EFT payments directly to SARS using internet banking services offered by: ABSA, AlBaraka, Access Bank (previously Grobank), Capitec, Discovery Bank, FNB, Grindrod Bank, HSBC, Investec, JP Morgan, Mercantile Bank, Nedbank, and Standard Bank.

title: How do you activate the VAT tax type on eFiling?
text: To activate the VAT tax type on eFiling, select the 'Activate' tick box to activate the VAT tax type on eFiling and click the Submit button to proceed.

title: What message will a vendor see if they try to submit a VAT201 return after the approved extension period has lapsed?
text: The following message will be displayed: 'The request for VAT201 cannot be processed as allowable period to submit VAT201 return after estimate has lapsed.'

title: What happens after SARS completes processing a request for extension?
text: Upon the completion of the request for extension by SARS, outcome letters will be issued and available on the Correspondence and the return Work Page on eFiling.

title: What forms of submission will SARS not accept for VAT201 Returns?
text: Faxed, emailed or photocopied VAT201 Returns printed from eFiling will not be accepted by SARS.

title: What should you do if a 'No Results' error message displays when viewing the VAT control table?
text: If a 'No Results' error message displays and the selected date range falls within the liability date, click the 'Refresh' button to obtain the most recent information from SARS.

title: What is the maximum date range allowed when viewing the VAT filing control table?
text: A maximum of 12 months is allowed when entering a start and end date range to view the VAT filing control table.

title: Where will you find your saved VAT201 Return if you opted to pay later?
text: If you opted to pay later you will find your saved VAT201 Return in the VAT201 Work Page.

title: How many copies of requested VATSOAs are kept on eFiling?
text: Only a copy of the last requested VATSOA will be kept on eFiling. The next requested VATSOA will replace the current VATSOA on eFiling.

title: What is the deadline for submitting relevant material after a section 95(1)(c) estimate assessment is issued, and what happens if this deadline is exceeded?
text: The deadline is 5 years plus 40 business days after the estimate assessment is issued. If a vendor attempts to submit relevant material after this deadline, the message displayed is: 'The deadline for submitting the relevant material has lapsed. Your request cannot be processed. Therefore, the Estimated Assessment issued to you for the tax period is now considered final'.

title: What are the three fields that must be completed in the Request for Extension screen?
text: The three fields that must be completed are: (i) Reason for Extension; (ii) Reason for extension description; and (iii) Date extended to.

title: When can a vendor request a VAT201 Return?
text: A vendor will be able to request the VAT201 Return immediately after the VAT tax type has successfully been activated.

title: What happens if you reduce the populated amount to be paid when making a VAT201 payment via eFiling?
text: If you reduce the populated amount to be paid, a message will be displayed. You can click 'OK' to proceed with the reduced payment or 'Cancel' to be routed back to the Payment Summary screen. If you click 'OK', you will receive a confirmation message.

title: Can input tax be deducted for expenditure on exempt supplies?
text: No, no input tax relating to the expenditure on exempt supplies may be deducted.

title: What information is pre-populated by SARS in the VAT201 Return?
text: The following fields will be pre-populated with the information at the disposal of SARS: Trading or Other Name; VAT Registration Number; Tax period for which the return is to be submitted; Payment Reference Number (PRN); and Diesel Rates to be applied for the applicable year in which the indicated tax period falls and according to the type of diesel activity for which the vendor is registered.

title: Where will outcome letters be issued after SARS completes the request for extension?
text: Outcome letters will be issued and available on the Correspondence and the return Work Page on eFiling.

title: Where should vendors declare output tax for standard rated supplies where VAT at 14% has been levied?
text: Vendors must declare the output tax in field 12 – 'Other and Imported Services' on the VAT201.

title: What should be entered in Field 17 of the VAT201 Return?
text: The VAT amount in respect of bad debts. Only the VAT amount in respect of irrecoverable debts on which VAT was levied during the sale should be filled in.

title: What are the possible statuses for VAT201 Returns that will be displayed on the menu?
text: The VAT201 Returns that will be displayed on the menu have the following statuses: (i) Submitted to SARS, (ii) Processed by SARS, (iii) Manually Submitted, and (iv) Selected for Audit.

title: What should you do if there are no bank details set up on eFiling when attempting to pay your VAT201 return?
text: If there are no bank details set up on eFiling, a screen will be displayed. You should click 'Setup banking information' to save bank details on eFiling to initiate payments.

title: How can you view your payment confirmation after completing a VAT201 return payment?
text: To view your payment confirmation, click on the 'Print Confirmation' button.

title: What should you do if you have already submitted the VAT201 return at a SARS office?
text: If you have already submitted the return at a SARS office, select the "Manually Submitted" option to update the eFiling return status.

title: What must be inserted in the Y box according to the VDP application instructions?
text: An "X" must be inserted in the "Y" box.

title: Which fields should vendors use to deduct input tax on imports regardless of whether VAT was charged at 14% or 15%?
text: Vendors are requested to use Field 14A and 15A of the VAT201 to deduct the input tax on all imports, irrespective of whether the VAT was charged at 14% or 15%.

title: What can you do if you need more time to submit relevant material?
text: You can request for an extension. Should your request for an extension be approved, you will be allowed to submit the relevant material.

title: What are the three actions vendors can now take on eFiling when SARS raises a section 95(1)(c) estimate assessment?
text: Vendors can now: (i) Submit relevant material after a section 95 estimate assessment was issued; (ii) Request for extension to submit supporting documents; (iii) Request Suspension of payment if a relevant Material case has been created.

title: What is the submission deadline difference between manual and eFiling submissions for VAT201 returns?
text: Vendors who submit their VAT201 returns manually must do so by the 25th of the month, while vendors who use eFiling have until the last business day of the month to submit VAT201 returns and payments. eFiling therefore affords the vendor additional days for submissions and payments.

title: What is the revision number of the GEN-ELEC-04-G01 guide?
text: The revision number is 11.

title: What are the two channels available for a vendor to submit a VAT201 Return?
text: The two channels available for a vendor to submit a VAT201 Return are eFiling and submission at a SARS Office.

title: What does the 'Submit VAT201' button allow you to do?
text: The 'Submit VAT201' button will allow you to submit the VAT201 Return to SARS.

title: What message is displayed if a vendor was not registered for VAT for the specified statement period?
text: The following message will be displayed: 'You were not registered for VAT for the statement period specified by you. Press OK to specify a different statement period or press Cancel to cancel the request.'

title: What must a vendor complete if their trading name has changed?
text: If the trading name or other name has changed, the vendor is required to inform SARS in writing.

title: What information will be pre-populated when revising a VAT201 Return?
text: When revising the original/previous submission of a VAT201 Return, the following fields will be pre-populated: Trading or Other Name, VAT Registration Number, Tax period for which the Return is to be made, the same Payment Reference Number (PRN) as in the original VAT201 Return, Diesel Rates to be applied for the applicable year, Financial data from the original/previous VAT201 Return submitted, and Contact details of the person who completed the original/previous submission.

title: What must be completed in Field 14A according to the VAT201 Return guide?
text: The permissible VAT amount of capital goods imported by you must be completed in Field 14A. The Customs Code field is mandatory.

title: What is the maximum time period during which a Vendor can request an extension to submit relevant material after an estimated assessment has been issued?
text: The Vendor can request an extension to submit relevant material up to 5 years and 40 business days after the original assessment for which the verification case was created.

title: What does Field 19 represent and how is it calculated?
text: Field 19 represents Total Input Tax and must be equal to the sum of Fields (14+14A+15+15A+16+17+18). This amount is automatically calculated on the VAT201 Return.

title: How do you activate VAT on eFiling if you are already registered?
text: Once logged into eFiling, select 'Organisation', 'Tax Types', and 'Manage Tax Types' from the side menu options under ORGANISATIONS to activate VAT on eFiling.

title: Can a vendor make a payment via eFiling before submitting the VAT201 Return?
text: No, a vendor will not be able to make a payment via eFiling prior to submitting the corresponding VAT201 Return via eFiling.

title: When should the 'Refresh' button be selected in the VAT filing process?
text: The 'Refresh' button should be selected if the vendor made a change to the category on registration or requested deregistration.

title: How can users access and view their filed VAT201 Returns on eFiling?
text: On eFiling, users will have the ability to search for and view their filed VAT201 Returns. The VAT201 Returns that are listed in the "Returns History" list will be accessible from the Returns Search menu option.

title: What happens after a VDP application has been evaluated by the VDP Unit?
text: Once the VDP application has been evaluated by the VDP Unit, a written agreement will be concluded between you and SARS to effect the necessary assessments.

title: What four concession types are provided in the current environment for administering diesel refunds?
text: The four concession types are: On Land, Offshore and Rail, Peak Power Plants, and Electricity Generators. The rates contained therein are used by vendors to convert the diesel litre usage to rands and to calculate the refund due based on the relevant rate.

title: What VAT rate do vendors need to levy on goods and services supplied from 1 April 2018?
text: Vendors need to levy VAT at the rate of 15% on all goods and services supplied, where those supplies qualify to be charged with VAT at the new rate.

title: What button should be clicked to proceed with submitting documents to SARS?
text: The "Submit to SARS" button should be clicked to proceed. Then the "OK" button on the confirmation message should be clicked to submit all documents to SARS.

title: What is the purpose of Field 32 in the Rail & Harbour Services section of the VAT201 Return?
text: Field 32 represents the amount of diesel in respect of eligible purchases (total purchases less non-eligible purchases). This field is mandatory if field 30 was completed and must be in full litres.

title: Can a Vendor request an extension for the same tax period multiple times while a case is in progress?
text: No. Once a Request for Extension has been submitted to SARS for a tax period, the Vendor will not be allowed to request extension for that same period again from the respective channels while the case is in progress or the date for which it has been approved has not lapsed.

title: What should vendors do before visiting a SARS office to submit their VAT201 Return?
text: All vendors should make an appointment on the SARS website before visiting a SARS office.

title: What payment methods are available to vendors for making VAT payments?
text: Vendors can make use of the following payment methods: eFiling, approved banking institutions, and EFT.

title: How do you remove bank details from the banking details page on eFiling?
text: To remove the bank details, click the 'Delete' button. Click 'OK' to confirm the deletion of the bank details or 'Cancel' to close the message. After you have confirmed the deletion, no bank details will be displayed on the banking details page on eFiling.

title: Can a vendor increase input tax deduction when revising a past tax period VAT201 Return?
text: No, the vendor will not be allowed to increase his input tax deduction when doing a revision on a past tax period. If the vendor wishes to deduct input tax attributable to a past tax period, a deduction can be made in the next tax period or any future tax period, only if the deduction is made within five years from the date of first entitlement to such input tax deduction.

title: What is the time limit for deducting input tax attributable to a past tax period in a future period?
text: If a vendor wishes to deduct input tax attributable to a past tax period, a deduction can be made in the next tax period or any future tax period, only if the deduction is made within five years from the date of first entitlement to such input tax deduction.

title: What should a vendor do if they made a change to the category on registration or requested deregistration?
text: A vendor should select the "Refresh" button if they made a change to the category on registration or requested deregistration.

title: What does it mean if Field 38 is less than zero on the VAT201 Return?
text: If Field 38 is less than zero, then the minus sign (-) must be added in the first block before the amount is entered in the field. This means a refund is due.

title: What format must be used to specify a tax period when requesting a VATSOA?
text: A tax period must be specified in the following format: (YYYYMM)

title: What action must be allowed for vendors regarding Section 95(1)(c) estimates instead of disputing them?
text: A vendor must be allowed to request a Suspension of Payment using the existing process instead of being able to dispute a Section 95(1)(c) estimate.

title: Where should the VDP application number come from?
text: The VDP application number as found on the VDP agreement must be completed.

title: What supporting documents are required when a vendor revises a VAT201 Return for the second time onwards?
text: When a vendor revises a VAT201 Return for the second time onwards (version 3), the vendor is required to submit supporting documents with the submission of the revised VAT201 Return to support the change made to the Return.

title: What message is displayed to the Vendor if no request for extension is received after the allowable time period has lapsed?
text: A message will be displayed stating: 'Request for Extension" can no longer be submitted as the allowable days to file the request has lapsed. Therefore, the Estimate Assessment issued to you for the tax period is now considered final'.

title: What document provides guidance on submitting a Dispute via eFiling?
text: The "Guide to submit a Dispute via eFiling - External Guide" available on the SARS website www.sars.gov.za provides assistance with the Dispute, including Suspension on Payment process.

title: What are the main purposes of the Voluntary Disclosure Programme (VDP)?
text: VDP seeks to: (A) Give applicants an opportunity to come clean by disclosing their tax defaults and/or exchange control contraventions and obtain relief if successful, (B) Establish a process within which the application criteria is transparent, and relief parameters clearly prescribed, and (C) Enhance the culture of compliance in both the tax and exchange control environments.

title: In what format is the VAT201 return displayed when you click on the VAT201 link?
text: The VAT201 will be displayed in HTML format.

title: When can a vendor upload relevant material for their VAT201 return?
text: Relevant material must only be uploaded if a letter is received from SARS requesting the vendor to submit relevant material. This letter will be issued via eFiling and can be accessed under 'SARS Correspondence'.

title: What should you do after selecting a bank from the Banking Product field?
text: After selecting the bank, the Account Number field will be displayed for completion. Complete this field and select 'Save'.

title: List five examples of standard rated supplies according to the guide.
text: Five examples of standard rated supplies are: Aircraft fuel, Building materials and services, Books and newspapers, Cigarettes/cold drinks/liquor, and Clothing.

title: Where does the container for uploading supporting documents appear after opening a SARS letter?
text: Once the letter is opened, another container will open on the VAT201 work page under 'SUPPORTING DOCUMENTS' from where the relevant material can be uploaded.
